title: Xystrolobos crispus
taxonomy: Xystrolobos crispus is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of South America, specifically in the Andes. Xystrolobos crispus belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and is also known as the Chilean lupine or Andean lupine. The plant is a perennial shrub that grows up to 1 meter in height, with yellow flowers and distinctive leaves that are often hairy and curled. As a member of the legume family, Xystrolobos crispus is a nitrogen-fixing plant that plays a crucial role in its ecosystem.
